promptHead:
You are a READ-ONLY auditor for the hmpsn.studio UI rebuild, Phase 0 (additive-parity functionality audit), undefined.
Repo: /Users/joshuahampson/CascadeProjects/asset-dashboard (branch ui-rebuild-phase-0 == post-Reconcile origin/staging HEAD). The UI Rebuild Kit lives at "/Users/joshuahampson/CascadeProjects/asset-dashboard/hmpsn studio Design System" — HTML docs; extract readable text with: text

structuredOutput.topic: Client-dashboard decision (Phase 0 GATE): new build per kit vs reuse/evolve existing 4-tab shell + The Issue

keyFindings.finding: The kit's own most recent audit (Client Surface Sweep, Migration Readiness dim 07) explicitly recommends AGAINST a redesign: "IA v2 — the four-tab shell built around The Issue — is the current, correct design… The current one already won… Finish the cutover. Don't redesign — retire." Choosing Option A (new dashboard) would overrule the kit with the kit.
keyFindings.evidence: hmpsn studio Design System/Client Surface Sweep.html §01–§05 (textutil extraction); endorses HEAD's Overview·Inbox·Results·Deep Dive(+Settings) and lists cutover cracks CL1–CL5

keyFindings.finding: The kit Mockup's Overview is already implemented at HEAD. The mockup's own source comments "OVERVIEW = THE ISSUE", and TheIssueClientPage implements it section-for-section (verdict headline, outcome count band, export one-pager, content-plan band, next bets, leads, loop footer). The mockup's other 4 tabs are literal placeholders ("Building this next — the Overview sets the pattern"). Option A would rebuild what exists dark behind flags.
keyFindings.evidence: Client Dashboard Mockup.html lines ~364-395 (TABS array, placeholder map, 'OVERVIEW = THE ISSUE' comment); src/components/client/the-issue/TheIssueClientPage.tsx:9-17,288-438; src/components/client/OverviewTab.tsx:158

keyFindings.finding: Client IA v2 (P1–P4) and The Issue client spine (P0–P1c) are COMPLETE at HEAD behind dark flags on staging; the only recorded remaining step is owner flag-ON validation → release to main. Flags: strategy-the-issue, the-issue-client-spine/measured-capture/return-hook/next-bets, client-ia-v2 — all default false.
keyFindings.evidence: FEATURE_AUDIT.md:8567 ("IA v2 single-site track (P1–P4) COMPLETE on staging behind the dark client-ia-v2 flag"); shared/types/feature-flags.ts:89-115,442-455,480

keyFindings.finding: The IA v2 tabs are slot-compositions of the legacy panels, not replacements: Deep Dive = PerformanceTab + HealthTab + StrategyTab + ContentPlanTab slots; Settings = BrandTab + PlansTab; Results = ROIDashboard(evergreen). Deleting legacy panels empties the new shell — so any rebuild path must either keep these components or deliberately re-home every one.
keyFindings.evidence: src/components/client/DeepDiveTab.tsx:33-41 (slot props); src/components/client/ResultsTab.tsx:15-21; FEATURE_AUDIT.md:8529; Client Surface Sweep §02 ("five legacy panels in a trench coat")

keyFindings.finding: Substantial shell chrome exists at HEAD with NO home in any kit client artifact and must survive by mandate: dual-mode client auth (shared password + client-user JWT, Turnstile, forgot/reset), inbox decisions/reviews/conversations routing + legacy alias map + bulk-approve/GBP/schema/content-solo machinery, TierGate/UpgradeModal/trial banners, Stripe PlansTab + SeoCartDrawer, ClientChatWidget, OnboardingWizard/Questionnaire/EmailCaptureGate, betaMode route variant, dark/light theme toggle, per-workspace seoClientView/analyticsClientView hide toggles, outcomeProvenance.ts money authority.

The kit contains THREE divergent client IAs: Plan (Overview·Performance·Strategy·Inbox), Mockup (Overview·Performance·Deep Dive·Inbox·Brand), portal.js (single light-theme microsite below a send boundary, operator-composes→client-receives lifecycle — a workflow change, not just UI). None matches the owner-ratified HEAD set (Overview·Inbox·Results·Deep Dive+Settings). This conflict cannot be resolved unilaterally.
keyFindings.evidence: Client Dashboard Plan.html §03; Client Dashboard Mockup.html TABS ~364; mockup/portal.js:1-15; Surface Model.html (/acme/portal, graduation path); clientDashboardNav.ts:44-52; FEATURE_AUDIT.md:8523

keyFindings.finding: Known cutover cracks at HEAD that any option must close: CL1 client-ia-v2 flag read independently at 3 sites (permanent fork risk); CL2 ROI vs Results dual panels (roi deliberately NOT aliased to results); CL3 orphaned tab components (SearchTab/AnalyticsTab dead aliases; RequestsTab/SchemaReviewTab/ContentTab unwired); CL4 *Tab naming/union implies 12 destinations vs 4 real; CL5 home differs by flag state. Also: client useFeatureFlag resolves GLOBAL flags only — no per-workspace client-UI pilot; the flip is all-or-nothing.
keyFindings.evidence: Client Surface Sweep §03; clientDashboardNav.ts; OverviewTab.tsx:151; FEATURE_AUDIT.md:8530; CLAUDE.md client useFeatureFlag note

keyFindings.finding: Recommendation: Option C (hybrid) — Stage 1 = Option B's cutover (validate flag-ON on staging, flip globally, retire legacy nav branch + flag forks, merge ROI→Results, delete orphans, rename slots) as the parity-safe foundation; Stage 2 = re-skin the ratified 4-tab shell with the 59-component design system per the Handoff Brief's per-surface template; Stage 3 = adopt portal.js's additive ideas (on-dashboard return hook, staged value band) as flagged phase-per-PR increments. Option A rejected: highest omission risk, re-buys built work, contradicts the kit's own audit and the owner's incremental-behind-flags direction.
keyFindings.evidence: docs/ui-rebuild/phase0/cross-client-dashboard.md §3–§4; Client Surface Sweep §05; UI Rebuild Handoff Brief Phase 0 gate text; memory project_p2_rebuild_prep_direction

recommendations: Adopt Option C (hybrid): finish the existing cutover first (Option B steps — the parity-safest, mostly-deletion stage), then re-skin the ratified 4-tab shell with the design system, then add portal.js ideas as flagged additive increments. Do not start Option A.

Before any flip: run the mandatory flag-ON real-render staging smoke (client-ia-v2 + strategy-the-issue + spine flags together) — this exact surface crashed once on a flag flip with all technical gates green.
Treat the kit Mockup's 5-tab set and the Plan's 4-tab set as superseded by the owner-ratified HEAD IA (Overview·Inbox·Results·Deep Dive+Settings) unless the owner explicitly overrules in Q2.
Keep outcomeProvenance.ts / shared/format-money.ts as the single money-render authority through any re-skin — the kit's own sweep calls it the model to extend, never bypass.

Sequence the cutover as its own PR wave (flag flip → branch deletion → ROI/Results merge → orphan deletion → *Tab→*Section rename), each with the client-tab-panel-lockstep contract test and flag-ON render guards kept green.

stopAndAsk.question: THE GATE: which option — A (new client dashboard per the kit), B (finish the cutover, no re-skin), or C (cutover → design-system re-skin → additive portal ideas; recommended)?
stopAndAsk.context: The Handoff Brief mandates no client-facing surface is built until this is decided. The kit's artifacts conflict internally: the Handoff Brief calls the prototype IA a decision, but the Client Surface Sweep says HEAD's IA v2 already won and to finish the cutover rather than redesign.
stopAndAsk.options: A / B / C (recommended: C)

stopAndAsk.question: Tab-set conflict: keep the owner-ratified Overview·Inbox·Results·Deep Dive(+Settings), or adopt the Mockup's Overview·Performance·Deep Dive·Inbox·Brand (Performance and Brand promoted back to top level)?
stopAndAsk.context: HEAD folds Performance into Deep Dive›Analytics and Brand into Settings (tournament-ratified 2026-06-21). The kit Mockup shows them as top-level tabs but only designed Overview.
stopAndAsk.options: Ratified 4+1 set (default) / Mockup 5-tab set / other

stopAndAsk.question: Theme: should the client surface become light-by-default (portal.js's 'bright trust surface' position), keep dark-default with the existing client-side toggle, or follow a platform-wide theme?
stopAndAsk.context: portal.js is light-by-design; HEAD client dashboard defaults dark with a localStorage toggle (.dashboard-light exists and works).
stopAndAsk.options: Light-by-default for clients / dark-default + toggle (current) / platform-wide

stopAndAsk.question: Is the portal.js send-boundary model (operator composes → send → client receives; 'a draft/queue/sent lifecycle, not a dashboard') in scope for this rebuild as a later flagged phase, or explicitly out of scope?
stopAndAsk.context: This is a workflow/product change beyond UI parity — it changes how client-facing content is produced, not just presented. The additive-parity mandate does not cover it without explicit owner scoping.
stopAndAsk.options: In scope (later flagged phase) / out of scope for this rebuild

stopAndAsk.question: Confirm the global-flip constraint: client useFeatureFlag resolves global flags only, so the IA v2/Issue flip moves ALL client workspaces at once (no per-workspace pilot). Proceed with staging validation → global flip → legacy retirement?
stopAndAsk.context: Per-workspace overrides gate the server and admin but never reach the client UI's render gate; a per-workspace pilot would show a confusing half-on state.
stopAndAsk.options: Yes, global flip after staging validation / no — build workspace-aware client gating first

stopAndAsk.question: Approve the ROI → Results merge (CL2): fold the month-over-month stat into evergreen Results, alias roi→results, retire the standalone ROIDashboard panel — changing what a bookmarked ?tab=roi client sees?
stopAndAsk.context: roi currently deliberately resolves to itself so flag-OFF bookmarks keep working; after the flip this becomes pure redundancy. This is the oldest open item in the client backlog.
stopAndAsk.options: Approve / keep both panels

stopAndAsk.question: Deferred items: schedule IA v2 P5 (multi-location) and the P2 'landing-in-shell polish' deferral inside this rebuild, or keep them deferred?
stopAndAsk.context: FEATURE_AUDIT.md:8536,8567 record both as owner-deferred; the rebuild is a natural slot but adds scope to the gate.
stopAndAsk.options: Fold into rebuild / keep deferred

stopAndAsk.question: Geo-grid: build the paid 49-point geo-grid rank scanner (new background job, per-node DataForSEO spend, new snapshot table), or ship Local Presence v1 on HEAD's existing single-point per-market posture data and defer the grid?
stopAndAsk.context: The 7x7 geo-grid is the mockup's centerpiece (local.js:74-94,156-168,285-357) but HEAD collects one local-pack check per market per keyword (local_visibility_snapshots) — no multi-point scans exist. The Parity Ledger marks this 'present' via LocalSeoVisibilityPanel, which is optimistic.
stopAndAsk.options: A) Defer geo-grid, render real posture data in a grid-like visual; B) Build the scanner first (cost gating like local-gbp); C) Ship both: posture view now, geo-grid behind a flag later

stopAndAsk.question: GBP Performance metrics (profile views/mo, calls & directions) on the hero card — in scope?
stopAndAsk.context: local.js:325-326 shows them; HEAD has no GBP Performance API integration or scope. Rendering them requires new OAuth scope + ingestion; rendering without data means invented client-facing numbers.

stopAndAsk.question: Where do HEAD's Visibility-tab capabilities live in the new IA?

stopAndAsk.question: Reinstate the 'Verified' badge the mockup shows, or keep claim status suppressed?
stopAndAsk.context: HEAD deliberately hides claim status: DataForSEO business_listings_search defaults is_claimed=true so unclaimed owners are never returned — the signal is unreliable (GbpReviewsPanel.tsx:44-47, P7 scaled review). The authenticated GBP connection could provide a reliable verified state, but only for connected workspaces.
stopAndAsk.options: A) Keep suppressed; B) Show only when derived from the authenticated GBP connection; C) Show as mockup (rejected by prior review)

stopAndAsk.question: Keep manual refresh triggers ('Refresh' local visibility + 'Refresh GBP & reviews') or move to scheduled refresh?
